blockingIssues.description: `useReducedMotion` assumes `window.matchMedia` and its listener APIs exist, so unsupported environments can throw before fallback UI renders.
The shader only falls back on initialization failures; runtime WebGL/context-loss failures leave the dead shader mounted instead of switching to the promised fallback path.
The new shared-chrome regression suite renders `<Home />` instead of `RootLayout`, so layout.tsx header/footer regressions are not actually covered.

Preview-eligibility assertions are not tied to the rendered `Preview Priority` field and do not verify Whale/Apex preview copy, so the promised per-tier preview-copy regression coverage is incomplete.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated `.factory/library/environment.md` to document that App Router favicon branding uses `src/app/icon.svg`.

Updated `.factory/library/environment.md` to reflect the current shadcn bootstrap split: `.factory/init.sh` only bootstraps missing artifacts, while `.factory/services.yaml` `install` still force-runs `shadcn init --force` and can rewrite tracked setup.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: When later mission guidance supersedes older feature text, add an explicit override note near the affected milestone/features so workers do not follow stale requirements (for example the retired ASCII layer brief versus the approved Radiant + film-grain stack).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The polish hero visual review found the feature brief still demanded an ASCII-video-inspired background while mission AGENTS.md and the frontend skill both banned ASCII and required a single Radiant shader plus film grain, which led directly to conflicting implementation behavior.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Tighten procedure compliance for browser-facing polish/visual work: require manifest-backed `web` service usage when available, explicit headed/GPU evidence for enhanced-visual validation when relevant, and `followedProcedure=false` whenever required research docs or test-first ordering were skipped.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple polish-release reviews found workers bypassed the manifest-backed service, validated only degraded/headless paths for heavy visuals, or added tests after implementation while still reporting `followedProcedure=true`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: For regression-only test features, explicitly direct workers to `.factory/library/testing.md` and forbid claiming shared-chrome coverage from page-only renders; require assertions to bind to the specific rendered field under test.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The cross-surface consistency review found a so-called RootLayout regression suite that only rendered `Home`, plus preview-copy assertions that matched generic values elsewhere in the card instead of the `Preview Priority` field.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify release-task behavior when baseline validation discovers fixable repository-local regressions, including whether workers should repair them before continuing or immediately return to the orchestrator.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The production-release review found the worker had to mark `followedProcedure=false` solely because the skill did not say whether self-healing baseline regressions was permitted during release validation.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add a new library note about RootLayout/shared-chrome coverage.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented in `.factory/library/testing.md`, which already states that page-only component tests do not validate shared layout chrome.
